zh.Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利
zh.Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Susan FitzAlan於大約1476年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親Sir William FitzAlan32歲，母親Elizabeth Haughstone28歲。她於大約1488年與Sir James Berkeley在英國格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利。

zh-hans.Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利
zh-hans.Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Susan FitzAlan于大约1476年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，她的父亲Sir William FitzAlan32岁，母亲Elizabeth Haughstone28岁。她于大约1488年与Sir James Berkeley在英国格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Susan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Suzan
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Susanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Susan FitzAlan was born about 1476, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Sir William FitzAlan, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Haughstone, was 28. She married Sir James Berkeley about 1488, in Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Susan FitzAlan est née environ 1476, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William FitzAlan, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Haughstone, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James Berkeley environ 1488, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Susan FitzAlan um 1476 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William FitzAlan 32 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Haughstone 28. Sie hat um 1488 in Gloucestershire, England Sir James Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Susan FitzAlan è nata il circa 1476 nel Arundel, Sussex, England, sua padre, Sir William FitzAlan, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Haughstone, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Sir James Berkeley circa 1488, nel Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Susan FitzAlanが約1476年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Sir William FitzAlanは32歳，母Elizabeth Haughstoneは28歳であった。約1488年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでSir James Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Susan FitzAlan(이)가 대략 1476년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William FitzAlan(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Haughstone(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1488년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 Sir James Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Susan FitzAlan nasceu aproximadamente 1476, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William FitzAlan, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Haughstone, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir James Berkeley aproximadamente 1488, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Susan FitzAlan родилась около 1476 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William FitzAlan было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Haughstone, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James Berkeley около 1488 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Susan FitzAlan nació aproximadamente 1476, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William FitzAlan, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Haughstone, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir James Berkeley aproximadamente 1488, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
